title: Diosma erecta
intro: Diosma erecta, also known as Cape starflower, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. Traditionally, Diosma erecta has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Diosma erecta include limonoids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Diosma erecta is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also incorporated into traditional remedies like Cape starflower oil, which is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Diosma erecta is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This plant is native to South Africa and belongs to the genus Diosma, which comprises around 60 species of evergreen shrubs. Diosma erecta is a small, shrub-like plant that grows up to 1 meter in height, characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow, linear leaves. The species Diosma erecta is further classified as Diosma erecta, which is a specific epithet that distinguishes it from other members of the genus. Through its classification, Diosma erecta is grouped with other plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections.
